steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage instructs historians: when a story has ‘sufficient authority,’ name who vouched for it; when it lacks authority, say so explicitly. The core duty is transparency about how trustworthy each source is.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — historians must avoid incomplete accounts: the passage is about credibility signaling, not completeness. Different point. ✗
  • Choice B — historians must not use non-credible sources: contradicts the passage, which says historians should include such accounts while flagging their low authority. ✗
  • Choice C — historians must indicate each source's trustworthiness: matches the passage's core instruction exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — historians must avoid eyewitness accounts: the passage never says this. Unsupported claim. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
